summary: Large Samus grapple item decomp/matching PR touching itsamusgrapple code and related Samus/item headers. The PR body explicitly notes many near-matches and duplicated code, suggesting semantic organization may help resolve remaining matches. Bot report shows 9 new matches and 13 unmatched-item improvements in main/melee/it/items/itsamusgrapple.

key_files.role: Primary implementation file; added many Samus grapple functions, helper inlines, typed item-var use, collision/animation logic, and matching tactics.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/it/items/itsamusgrapple.c added 1018 deleted 30 hunks 7
diff: includes added helper inlines samus_grapple_fighter_compare, samus_grapple_init_link, samus_grapple_setup_pos, samus_grapple_anim, samus_grapple_state_sync
text_corpus: bot report lists new matches and improvements in main/melee/it/items/itsamusgrapple

key_files.role: Item header typing; changed Samus grapple item vars x0/x4 from f32 to ItemLink* and added itSamusGrappleAttributes struct.
evidence_refs: diff: itSamusGrapple_ItemVars x0/x4 changed to ItemLink*
diff: added itSamusGrappleAttributes fields x0 through xA0

key_files.role: Function prototype surface; included itCharItems.h and replaced multiple UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with typed signatures.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/it/items/itsamusgrapple.h added 24 deleted 13 hunks 3
diff: it_802B7160, it_802B743C, it_802B900C, it_802B91C4, it_802B9328, it_802B99A0, it_802B9CE8, it_802B9FD4, it_802BA194, it_802BA2D8, it_802BA3BC, it_802BA5DC, it_802BA760 prototypes typed

key_files.role: Samus fighter type updates; refined ftSs_DatAttrs fields and added a provisional State7 motion-var struct.
evidence_refs: diff: ftSs_DatAttrs xA0 and xB0 changed from UNK_T to s32; xCC changed from UNK_T to f32
diff: added ftSamus_State7Vars with @todo Proper state name

what_changed: Converted substantial parts of src/melee/it/items/itsamusgrapple.c from placeholder comments to C implementations, including it_802B743C, fn_802B7E34, fn_802B805C, fn_802B8384, fn_802B8524, fn_802B8684, fn_802B8814, fn_802B895C, fn_802B8B54, it_802BACC4, and others visible in the diff excerpt.

Introduced local static inline helpers for repeated Samus grapple operations: fighter motion comparison, ItemLink initialization, gravity calculation, JObj position setup, animation over link chains, state sync, and smaller function-specific inline chunks.
Replaced incorrect or generic item-var access in shown sites from linkhookshot to samusgrapple and changed itSamusGrapple_ItemVars x0/x4 to ItemLink*.
Added itSamusGrappleAttributes with many float, integer, joint, anim, matanim, and shapeanim fields used by itsamusgrapple.c.
Updated itsamusgrapple.h to include itCharItems.h and provide typed prototypes instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for many grapple functions.
Refined ftSamus DatAttrs field types at xA0, xB0, and xCC and added a provisional ftSamus_State7Vars entry with an explicit TODO for proper state naming.
Bot report recorded matched code progress of +3336 bytes, 9 new matches, and 13 improvements in unmatched items for main/melee/it/items/itsamusgrapple.
smart_moves: The PR uses typed ItemLink* fields and an itSamusGrappleAttributes struct instead of leaving Samus grapple item data as generic f32/UNK access, aligning with typed-field recovery.

Repeated patterns such as JObj matrix position extraction and animating all grapple links were factored into local helpers, which matches the PR author's note that semantic organization could reduce duplicated near-match code.
Header prototypes were updated alongside recovered bodies, reducing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and making call surfaces more truthful.
The diff keeps uncertain semantics visible via address-style names and an explicit @todo for the newly added Samus State7 motion-vars rather than inventing a state name.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a PR contains many near-matches and duplicated item logic, first recover the semantic data model and repeated operations; helper inlines and typed structs can make remaining matching work more organized.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author says there are lots of near-matches and duplicated code and suspects semantic understanding would help organize it
diff: helper inlines added for state sync, setup_pos, anim, init_link, and motion comparison
diff: itSamusGrappleAttributes and ItemLink* item vars added

decomp_lessons.lesson: Typed item-var union arms matter: using samusgrapple ItemLink* fields instead of borrowing linkhookshot or f32-shaped fields makes the source clearer and enables typed function signatures.
evidence_refs: diff: xDD4_itemVar.linkhookshot.x0 replaced with xDD4_itemVar.samusgrapple.x0 in shown itsamusgrapple.c sites
diff: itSamusGrapple_ItemVars x0/x4 changed from f32 to ItemLink*
diff: prototypes now take ItemLink* and itSamusGrappleAttributes*

decomp_lessons.lesson: If recovered bodies prove function signatures, update the owning header rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
evidence_refs: diff: itsamusgrapple.h changed multiple UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes to concrete return and parameter types
decomp_standards: truthful-headers-and-includes

naming_conventions.convention: Use subsystem-prefixed local helper names for recovered repeated Samus grapple behavior, such as samus_grapple_setup_pos, samus_grapple_anim, and samus_grapple_state_sync.
evidence_refs: diff: added static inline helpers with samus_grapple_ prefix

naming_conventions.convention: Keep address-style names such as it_802B743C and fn_802B805C when semantics are not fully proven; add explicit TODOs for uncertain state names instead of guessing.
evidence_refs: diff: recovered functions retain address-style names
diff: ftSamus_State7Vars has comment '@todo Proper state name'
decomp_standards: conservative-naming

naming_conventions.convention: Use common short local role names such as ip, fp, gobj, jobj, link, attrs, pos, and vel where roles are clear.
evidence_refs: diff: itsamusgrapple.c functions use Item* ip, Fighter* fp, HSD_JObj* jobj, ItemLink* link, itSamusGrappleAttributes* attrs, Vec3 pos

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used narrow static inline helpers to share repeated source shapes while preserving match-sensitive operations such as JObj matrix setup, link-chain animation, and state updates.
evidence_refs: diff: samus_grapple_setup_pos calls PSMTXIdentity, HSD_JObjSetupMatrix, and reads jobj->mtx translation
diff: samus_grapple_anim animates GET_JOBJ(gobj) and iterates link->prev
diff: samus_grapple_state_sync switch increments fp->fv.ss.x2240 based on input bits

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK and explicit padding locals were used in multiple functions, indicating stack-shape steering for near matches; evidence supports use in the PR but not a general style recommendation.
evidence_refs: diff: PAD_STACK(20) in fn_802B7E34
diff: PAD_STACK(16) in fn_802B805C
diff: PAD_STACK(28) and u8 _pad locals in several functions
decomp_standards: matching-tactics-need-evidence

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Switch-based collision/motion result handling was used for grapple physics routines, with cases for bounce, floor mirror/normal handling, and state transitions.
evidence_refs: diff: fn_802B805C switch on it_802B9328 result
diff: fn_802B8384 switch on it_802B99A0 result

accepted_candidate_records.title: Semantic recovery can organize duplicated near-match item code
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2328 is direct evidence that a cluster of near-matching, duplicated item logic may benefit from recovering typed data structures and shared helper operations before trying to close every function individually.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author notes near-matches and duplicated code
diff: helper inlines and itSamusGrappleAttributes added
text_corpus: bot report shows multiple new matches/improvements

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed item-vars and header prototypes reinforce each other
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2328, changing Samus grapple item vars to ItemLink* and adding itSamusGrappleAttributes coincided with replacing many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with typed prototypes in itsamusgrapple.h.
evidence_refs: diff: itSamusGrapple_ItemVars x0/x4 changed from f32 to ItemLink*
diff: itsamusgrapple.h prototypes typed

source_update_candidates.title: itsamusgrapple.c Samus grapple decomp/matching cluster
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2328 is evidence that src/melee/it/items/itsamusgrapple.c contains a Samus grapple item matching cluster with many link-chain, JObj animation/matrix, collision, and fighter-state interactions; work in this file may require coordinated updates to itCharItems.h, itsamusgrapple.h, and ftSamus/types.h.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: itsamusgrapple.c, itCharItems.h, itsamusgrapple.h, ftSamus/types.h changed
diff: helpers and prototypes reference ItemLink, Fighter, itSamusGrappleAttributes, HSD_JObj, collision helpers

source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scoped context for future curator indexing; keep as candidate because current intake should not directly update path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not generalize PAD_STACK from near-match PRs without verifier evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2328 uses PAD_STACK and padding locals in several near-matching functions, but the evidence only supports narrow use in this PR. If curated, this should reinforce the existing matching-tactics standard that stack padding requires explicit match/regression evidence and should not become ordinary style.
evidence_refs: diff: PAD_STACK appears in fn_802B7E34, fn_802B805C, fn_802B8384, fn_802B8524 and other shown functions
decomp_standards: matching-tactics-need-evidence
source_update_candidates.reason: Candidate is framed as reinforcement only; PR evidence does not justify a new broad standard.

rejection_notes: Do not infer the proper name of ftSamus_State7Vars; the diff explicitly leaves a TODO.
Do not promote PAD_STACK or padding locals as accepted general style from this PR; evidence only shows they were used here.
Do not claim all itsamusgrapple functions matched; bot report lists specific new matches and specific near-match improvements, not complete TU closure.
Do not infer reviewer intent, because there are no review comments or reviews in the loaded slice.
